causal_question: For cities that switched to single-member district elections in 2018, how did the switch affect the natural log of multifamily units permitted between 2017 and 2019 compared to otherwise-similar cities that had not switched by 2019, adjusting for population, homeownership and vacancy rates, median home value and income, and prior minority electoral success?
vague_question: For cities that switched to district-based city council elections in 2018, did the number of new multifamily housing permits change in the years around that switch?
